# 2026-03-10

## Testing Strategies Discussion (14:08-14:27)

[[alice]] asked about integration tests vs contract tests for microservices. Sparked discussion on [[integration-testing-with-testcontainers]].

**[[alice]]'s experience:** Burned by mock drift — tests green but production broke. Using testcontainers (Postgres per suite), 200 test files, hit connection pool exhaustion with database-per-file approach.

**[[bob]]'s approach:** Schema-per-file with shared testcontainers instance. Parallel execution with clean isolation. Layered strategy: unit (fast, no I/O) → integration (testcontainers) → small set of end-to-end.

**[[carol]]** joined for contract testing perspective. Uses Pact/PactFlow for consumer-driven contracts between frontend and API. `can-i-deploy` CI check blocks breaking changes.

### End-to-End Test Discussion

Consensus: keep E2E count low (~10-12). [[bob]] cut from 60 to 12, CI went from 50 minutes to 8. Most of the 48 removed tests duplicated what integration tests already covered.

## Resources Shared

- [[parallel-testing-blog]] by [[bob]] (14:17) - schema-per-file parallel integration testing
- [[testcontainers-patterns]] by [[bob]] (14:27) - GitHub repo, testing patterns collection
